# Scorched-Earth ErdĹ‘s Sweep â€” Turn 88

This is turn 88. Turns 1 through 87 are consolidated in `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` (attached). Re-read it before answering.
Also re-read `mega_prompt.md` and `problem_statement.md`.

CRITICAL â€” use ONLY the files attached to THIS message. Do NOT open, import, retrieve, or cite any file from your File Library / previously uploaded files, even one named similarly (e.g. another `turns_historyâ€¦md`). Other unrelated problems use similarly named history files in the same account, and importing one will silently switch you onto the wrong problem. If the attached `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` appears missing, do NOT continue from partial context. Reply only with `MISSING_REQUIRED_ATTACHMENT` and name the missing file(s); never substitute a library file.

If this turn drifts into hammering one specific case or sub-instance, step back and ask whether the case is revealing a structural pattern that would close many cases at once. Prefer arguments whose conclusion is a uniform statement.

End this response with ESTABLISHED / GAP / NEXT + tag.
Continue with Turn 88.

## Turn 88 â€” the fixed-frame theorem now uses only ordinary Thompson criticality

The candidate remains unchanged:

```text
sigma,tau,x0,x1

sigma2 = sigma^40491355905
u0 = (x0*tau)^40491355905
u1 = (x1*tau)^40491355905
d0 = u0*x0^-1
z0 = x0^sigma2
c0 = d0^-1*z0^-1*d0*z0

x0^2*x1^4*u1^-1*(x1^sigma)*c0
```

The tame relation is implicit. Mathematically, the exponent is the genuine profinite idempotent \(\omega _2\); \(40491355905\) is only its prescribed verifier residue. 

Turn 87 proved exactly the reduction needed to remove the special small-exponent critical-subgroup axiom. 

### Critical-block input

Use ordinary Thompson criticality in the form: every finite \(2\)-group \(K\) has \(C\char K\) such that

\[
C/Z(C)\ \text{is elementary abelian},\qquad [C,K]\le Z(C),
\]

and

\[
\ker\!\bigl(\operatorname{Aut}(K)\to\operatorname{Aut}(C)\bigr)
\]

is a \(2\)-group.

Set

\[
D=\Omega _2(C)=\langle c\in C:c^4=1\rangle .
\]

The Turn-87 argument gives

\[
D^4=1,\qquad [D,K]\le Z(D),\qquad
\ker\!\bigl(\operatorname{Aut}(K)\to\operatorname{Aut}(D)\bigr)
\ \text{is a \(2\)-group}. \tag{88.1}
\]

The essential detection step is now self-contained. If an odd-order automorphism \(\alpha\) of \(C\) fixes \(D\), it first fixes \(Z(C)\) by the abelian \(\Omega _2\)-detection lemma. Then

\[
\nu:C/Z(C)\longrightarrow Z(C)^2/Z(C)^4,\qquad
xZ(C)\longmapsto x^4Z(C)^4
\]

has kernel \(DZ(C)/Z(C)\). Hence

\[
(\bar\alpha-1)(C/Z(C))\subseteq DZ(C)/Z(C),
\qquad
(\bar\alpha-1)^2=0.
\]

Thus \(\bar\alpha^2=1\); odd order forces \(\bar\alpha=1\). Since \(\alpha\) fixes both \(Z(C)\) and \(C/Z(C)\), it is a central shear of exponent at most two, and odd order again forces \(\alpha=1\). This proves (88.1).

### Insertion into the minimal active block

Fix a tame frame

\[
\alpha:T_{\mathrm{tame}}\twoheadrightarrow G/L
\]

and induct on the marked wild \(2\)-kernel \(L\).

Suppose not every chief factor of \(L\) is scalar. Choose

\[
S\triangleleft P\triangleleft G,\qquad P/S\simeq V,
\]

where the factors below \(S\) are scalar and \(V\) is the first nontrivial simple factor. Choose \(K\triangleleft G\), \(K\le P\), minimally with \(KS=P\).

The relevant odd Hall lift \(\widetilde N\) centralizes \(S\) but acts nontrivially on

\[
K/(K\cap S)\simeq V.
\]

By (88.1), it acts nontrivially on \(D\). Therefore \(D\nleq S\). Since \(D\char K\triangleleft G\), simplicity of \(P/S\) gives \(DS=P\), and minimality of \(K\) forces

\[
D=K.
\]

Consequently,

\[
K^4=1,\qquad [K,K]\le Z(K).
\]

Thus

\[
R:=\Phi(K)=K^2[K,K]
\]

is elementary abelian and central in \(K\). It need not be central in all of \(G\); this is harmless because the Fourier argument uses only \(G/R\)-invariant characters of \(R\), whose scalar pushouts are central.

Put

\[
M=K/R,\qquad T_0=(K\cap S)/R.
\]

The minimality argument gives

\[
T_0=\operatorname{rad}_{\mathbf F_2[G/K]}(M),\qquad
M/T_0\simeq V,\qquad
(M^\vee)^{G/K}=0. \tag{88.2}
\]

### The two lifting stages

Finite-Heisenberg deformation duality applied to the elementary module \(M\) yields

\[
\operatorname{Ob}_\rho(M)^\vee\simeq(M^\vee)^{G/K}=0.
\]

Hence every generated lower map lifts through \(M\), with exactly

\[
2^{2\dim M}
\]

relation-compatible lifts on both the candidate and local sides. Proper-image contributions have smaller marked wild kernel and are handled inductively.

It remains to lift through \(R\). Let

\[
\mathcal D=(R^\vee)^{G/R}.
\]

For each fixed-frame lower map \(\rho\), finite-Heisenberg duality identifies \(\mathcal D\) with the full character group of its \(R\)-valued relation-obstruction space. Fourier inversion therefore gives

\[
\#\{\rho:o(\rho)=0\}
=
\frac1{|\mathcal D|}
\sum_{\lambda\in\mathcal D}
\bigl(2m_\lambda-|X|\bigr), \tag{88.3}
\]

where \(m_\lambda\) counts lower maps for which the scalar pushout obstruction along \(\lambda\) vanishes.

For \(\lambda\ne0\), the simple-head determinant lemma says that the pushout quadratic map vanishes on \(T_0\), its polar form vanishes on \(T_0\times M\), and it descends to a nonzero nonsingular determinant form on \(V\).

If the filtration-one edge across \(T_0\) is nonzero, deformation duality gives an exact half-split. Since

\[
T_0\le \Phi(G/R),
\]

all those lifts are already surjective.

If the edge is zero, transgression exposes a split \(V\)-layer, marking-safe shearing removes mixed edge terms, and the problem becomes the pure determinant calculation. The candidate Wallâ€“Arf form and the local Kummer/deep-unit form have the same zero count: minus type when \(V\) is unramified and hyperbolic when \(V\) is ramified.

Thus every summand in (88.3) agrees. Once the \(R\)-obstruction vanishes, both sources have the same lift multiplicity, and every lift is surjective because \(R=\Phi(K)\).

Therefore, for every complete tame frame and every scalar decoration,

\[
N_{\Gamma_{A_2}}(G,L;\alpha,\theta,\psi)
=
N_{G_{\mathbf Q_2}}(G,L;\alpha,\theta,\psi). \tag{88.4}
\]

Summing over tame frames, taking trivial decoration, and setting \(L=O_2(G)\) gives equality of raw surjection counts for every finite target. The specialized small-exponent Thompson proposition is no longer an independent hypothesis: it is a consequence of ordinary Thompson criticality.

**ESTABLISHED**: Inserted the Turn-87 \(\Omega _2(C)\) refinement into the fixed-tame-frame induction. The critical block now follows from ordinary Thompson criticality alone; the minimal active subgroup is uniformly class two of exponent four, its Frattini subgroup is elementary, and the nonsemisimple/Fourier/determinant lifting argument closes every wild-kernel configuration.

**GAP**: The private 5402-group verifier remains unrun. A final publication note still needs a precise citation for ordinary Thompson criticality and polished consolidation of the finite-Heisenberg and Wallâ€“Arf/local-Kummer lemmas.

**NEXT**: Produce the standalone proof note with the theorem above and append the unchanged verifier block for submission.
